target_semantics: Start in normal state and support a reliable normal-to-insert-to-normal editing roundtrip.

target_semantics: Use Space as leader in normal and visual states and resolve every declared Lem leader chord to its intended command.
divergence: none
notes: Command-level parity is tracked separately where implementations diverge.

target_semantics: Send the current region or buffer to the LLM from insert state with C-c i.
divergence: unassessed

target_semantics: C-u in insert state deletes back to indentation without leaving insert state.
divergence: none

target_semantics: C-n and C-p remain ordinary logical-line movement outside completion-specific keymaps.
divergence: none

target_semantics: Provide the configured global window, duplicate, recent-file, grep, find-name, and buffer-list workflows on their expected bindings.

notes: The TUI suite exercises M-j, M-g r, M-s f, and C-x C-b; M-o and M-s g remain source-verified.

target_semantics: Provide mode-local Claude Code, Notmuch payment-email, Elfeed archive, Salta, and project outline bindings.
divergence: No project-local consult-outline binding and Notmuch payment-email bridge is not reproduced.

target_semantics: Support ys, ds, cs, visual S, and padded delimiter surround editing while retaining native operators.

target_semantics: Provide visible-scope two-character snipe motions, repeat, and operator integration.
divergence: Incremental highlighting is absent and f/t are not replaced by snipe.

target_semantics: Use gc as a normal-state operator and visual-state action with language-appropriate line comments.

target_semantics: Repeated SPC v expands the selection through useful syntactic units.

target_semantics: Enable delimiter-safe Lispyville-style operators, atom movement, slurp/barf, and configured structural transforms in all configured Lisp-family buffers.

target_semantics: Jump to visible lines, characters, and symbols with Avy-style target selection.

target_semantics: Show distinct terminal cursor colors or shapes for insert, normal, visual, replace, and Emacs states.
divergence: No state-specific cursor appearance configuration is present.

target_semantics: Retain Vim linewise operators, counts, dot-repeat, and register semantics alongside custom operator dispatch.
divergence: none
notes: Scope is the explicitly tested operator and register behavior.

target_semantics: Use spaces for indentation with tab width four and allow mode-specific indentation rules.

target_semantics: Trim trailing whitespace only on lines touched during editing when saving a programming buffer.
divergence: none

target_semantics: Automatically insert balanced delimiter pairs globally.
divergence: Pair insertion, counts, escapes, local balance reuse, whitespace skipping, prompts, and Paredit integration work; full forward balance scanning across forms, global adjacent-pair Backspace, and zero-result prompt recovery remain absent.
notes: The real TUI exercises Fundamental, Python, Lisp/Paredit, completion, numeric prefixes, odd/even escapes, balanced and unmatched adjacent delimiters, whitespace/newline skipping, read-only buffers, undo, Vi replace/visual isolation, and mode hook preservation.

target_semantics: Typing replaces an active selection.
divergence: none
notes: The exact claim is scoped to the target: ordinary insertion replaces forward or reverse nonempty Emacs-style regions. Openers wrap instead; the TUI verifies mark state, orientation, zero-width marks, one-step undo, read-only buffers, Paredit precedence, and Vi visual isolation. Lem deliberately escapes an unmatched embedded quote to keep a wrapped Lisp string valid rather than reproduce Lispy's raw interior quote.

target_semantics: Provide built-in undo and redo with raised history limits plus a navigable visual undo tree.
divergence: Linear undo and redo exist, but SPC u is unbound; the first edit after undo destroys the abandoned redo branch, so a truthful vundo requires a core retained-tree history before its UI and limits can be ported.
notes: A linear popup would misrepresent the configured vundo 2.4.0 behavior.

target_semantics: M-j duplicates the active region when present, otherwise the current line.
divergence: Visual-block or rectangle duplication is explicitly unsupported.
notes: The TUI suite covers Emacs-compatible unterminated-EOF newlines, exact point retention, one-step undo, forward and reverse VISUAL selections, V-LINE retention, and Paredit's structural M-j precedence.

target_semantics: Fill the current paragraph and optionally auto-fill while typing at the configured column.
divergence: none
notes: Exact is limited to the tested plain-text paragraph and buffer-local toggle semantics.

target_semantics: Disable lockfiles, backup files, and auto-save files.
divergence: none
notes: The real ncurses gate edits and waits without saving, proves the visited file is unchanged, verifies auto-save remains inactive, and rejects Lem- and Emacs-style backup, lock, and auto-save sidecars.

target_semantics: Toggle visual line wrapping without changing the underlying buffer.

target_semantics: Keep file completion on partial path-component matching while applying learned candidate ranking.

target_semantics: Annotate completion candidates with useful contextual metadata.
divergence: M-x preserves keybinding annotations, but broader Marginalia category annotations are absent.

target_semantics: Offer automatic in-buffer completion after a 3-character prefix and 0.2-second idle delay in terminal buffers.

target_semantics: Expand private and community snippets globally with placeholder and field navigation through Tab.

target_semantics: Find project files, grep a project, switch projects, and switch among project buffers.
divergence: Consult live preview and identical candidate metadata remain absent; non-Git marker projects retain Lem's broader root semantics, and the shared upstream history file is not transaction-safe across concurrent processes.

target_semantics: Offer context-sensitive actions for the current candidate or object and integrate those actions with search results.

notes: The real ncurses suite verifies exact normal/visual leader and completion-local bindings, target-specific dispatch and cancellation, forward/reverse region priority, URL copy, relative and property-backed files, identifier definition/reference delegation, native-menu delegation, completion copy-without-accept and exact-once acceptance, peek locations, stale-target safety, and reload idempotence; LSP code-action gating, external opening, and buffer actions remain source-inspected.

target_semantics: Edit grep result matches in place and write changes back to source files.

target_semantics: Apply case-smart, space-separated Orderless matching to in-buffer candidates and expose the Corfu separator workflow.

target_semantics: M-s f prompts for a directory and wildcard, finds matching names recursively, and keeps navigable results available after visiting a match.

notes: The real TUI verifies both prompts, asynchronous results, files and directories, literal Unix metacharacters, control-character escaping, argv safety, buffer ownership, read-only persistence, Vi Return, and q.

target_semantics: Run community snippet conditions, command snippets, backquoted Emacs Lisp, and field transforms with Yasnippet semantics.
divergence: The 144 executable or conditional pinned definitions are classified as unavailable and their embedded code is never evaluated.

target_semantics: Interpret LSP completion items whose insertTextFormat is Snippet and preserve their placeholder session after acceptance.

target_semantics: Resolve an accepted LSP completion item lazily and apply direct or resolved additionalTextEdits without corrupting its primary insertion.
divergence: Acceptance is synchronously bounded at two seconds, only additionalTextEdits is advertised and imported, invalid or overlapping batches are skipped rather than reproduced with Eglot's order-dependent behavior, and Lem has no native change group for rollback after an arbitrary throwing mutation hook.
notes: The real ncurses gate verifies deferred exact-once resolve, timeout-state cleanup, original-primary preservation, direct and resolved edits around a length-changing snippet, literal $1 additional text, UTF-16 composite ranges, read-only preflight, one-step undo, and fail-closed invalid or overlapping batches.

notes: SPC p g converts Emacs regexp syntax, passes it after --, searches the exact tracked-plus-untracked project file set in bounded argv batches, and renders editable peek results off the editor thread.

target_semantics: Automatically revert changed files and persist places, prompt history, the kill ring, and searches.
divergence: Command-triggered polling replaces Emacs idle timers and filesystem notifications; same-metadata rewrites above 16 MiB are detected on explicit save but not periodic scans; transactional reload snapshots are capped at 64 MiB; saved places cover local files but not directory buffers; non-file buffers require explicit adapters; prompt persistence is a reviewed default-deny subset; oversized entries remain live but are omitted; stale processes can resurrect cleared histories.
notes: The 45-check ncurses gate covers safe clean and dirty external changes, large/deleted/new-file stale-save guards, Save As races, modified quit refusal, fresh-process file positions, separate literal/regexp history, prompt privacy/live caps, physical Vi-aware kill order, bounded non-evaluating state reads, private modes, failure-safe exit, and stale concurrent-process merging. Recent files remain split out under MISC-013.

target_semantics: Compile configuration ahead of time and avoid writing compiled artifacts into the source tree.
divergence: The Nix wrapper redirects ASDF outputs to cache and loads on demand rather than AOT native-compiling every source.
notes: none

target_semantics: Persist a deduplicated MRU of at most 300 recent files across editor processes and open it with M-g r.
divergence: none
notes: A pre-seeded oversized history is trimmed newest-first, index-reset, rewritten, and reloaded; reopening an older file then moves one entry to the front, and Return opens it.

target_semantics: Automatically persist modified bookmarks at exit and restore them in a fresh editor process.
divergence: Pinned Lem provides explicit bookmark save and load commands but lem-yath does not automatically invoke them or isolate and merge bookmark state.
notes: Bookmark creation and jumping remain available on SPC b m and SPC RET; this row tracks the configured Emacs lifecycle rather than the commands themselves.

target_semantics: Treat daily modal editing, leader bindings, completion, navigation, editing defaults, structural editing, and UI basics as the first acceptance tier.
divergence: Priority classification is planning metadata rather than runtime behavior.
notes: The behavior rows above carry the actionable dispositions and evidence.

target_semantics: Treat language tooling, VCS, notes, debugging, and AI entry points as the second acceptance tier.
divergence: Priority classification is planning metadata rather than runtime behavior.
notes: Rows may use P1 or P2 based on current daily impact while preserving the inventory tier as source context.

target_semantics: Track bespoke applications and host-gated integrations explicitly even where Lem has no counterpart.
divergence: Priority classification is planning metadata rather than runtime behavior.
notes: Gaps remain visible instead of disappearing from package-centric summaries.

target_semantics: Do not port a Doom modeline that is referenced only by a dead customization variable and is not installed.
divergence: The reference has no active target semantics.
notes: Keep dead configuration out of implementation scope.

target_semantics: Assess default-only language packages before claiming their modes or workflows are required.
divergence: unassessed
notes: Explicitly separates package installation from configured behavior.

target_semantics: Assess default-only Org and publishing packages before adding new port scope.
divergence: unassessed
notes: No behavior should be inferred solely from package presence.

target_semantics: Assess nov and pgmacs defaults independently from the explicit app workflows already tracked.
divergence: unassessed
notes: Pgmacs has a narrower explicit Lem implementation; Nov remains a gap in APP-004.
